lenses.guidance: The 'three days' refrain (vv 12, 18, 20) structures the prison scene — restoration on the third day for the cupbearer, judgement on the third day for the baker. The numeric repetition rehearses what gen 41 will hear from Pharaoh.

'Do not interpretations belong to God?' (v 8) names where wisdom finally lives. Trust God with the meaning of your life when the dream is dark; respond by serving where you are placed, even in prison.

The cupbearer's forgetting (v 23) extends Joseph's wait by two more years (cf. 41:1) — covenant timing diverges from human timing. The promise advances on God's calendar, never the prisoner's, and the delay turns out to be preparation.
